steps.title: Mark the introductory phrase
steps.content: By supplying oil, grapes, and grain to societies that lacked these valuable items is an introductory phrase. It should be followed by a comma before the main clause.

steps.title: Reject stronger breaks
steps.content:
• Choice A uses a semicolon, but the words before it are not an independent clause.
  • Choice C omits the comma needed after the introductory phrase.
  • Choice D uses a period, creating a fragment before the main clause.

steps.title: Use the comma
steps.content: A comma after items correctly separates the introductory phrase from the main clause about the Roman Empire growing in wealth and power. The answer is B.
